content:
# Session 11 (iter-011) — review

## Metadata
- Prover lanes: 2 (opus) — FBC `FlatBaseChange.lean`, SNAP `SectionGradedRing.lean`.
- Sorry count: **FBC 5→5, SNAP 6→6** (net 0). NO sorry-elimination this iter, BUT real structural
  progress on both routes (5 new closed decls total; one monolithic kernel-bomb foundation
  decomposed to a single named residual). 0 new axioms (blueprint-doctor: clean; lean_verify: no
  new sorryAx beyond the tracked residuals). Both files cold-build exit 0 (FBC 8318 jobs; SNAP
  2441 jobs) — no kernel timeout.
- blueprint-doctor (iter-011): **0 findings** (all chapters `\input`'d, all `\ref`/`\uses`
  resolve, no axioms).

## FBC `FlatBaseChange.lean` — foundation decomposed to ONE named cast residual (closest-to-done)
- **`globalSectionsIso_hom_comp3_specMap_appTop` (NEW) — SOLVED.** Body
  `globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop (φ ≫ ρ)` — the 3-fold square is just the single-map
  lemma at the composite.
- **`gammaPushforwardIso_hom_apply` / `gammaPushforwardIso_inv_apply` (NEW) — SOLVED** (`:= rfl`).
  Every `gammaPushforwardIso` constituent is identity-on-carrier. These collapse wrappers WITHOUT
  `change`/defeq (the kernel-bomb route). **Coverage debt: no blueprint entry — see recs.**
- **`gammaPushforwardNatIso_comp` — own body CLOSED** (was the iter-009/010 monolithic
  kernel-bomb `sorry`). Winning move:
  `apply Iso.ext; apply NatTrans.ext; funext N; simp only [<structural whiskering/associator
  lemmas>, NatIso.ofComponents_hom_app]; exact gammaPushforwardIso_comp φ ρ N`. The structural
  `simp` collapses the RHS pasting (associators → `𝟙`, absorbed by cheap `id_comp`, NOT sheaf
  whnf) to a per-component term, bounding the kernel cost. Transitively gated on
  `gammaPushforwardIso_comp`.
- **`gammaPushforwardIso_comp` (NEW) — PARTIAL, 1 isolated `sorry` (L726).** The giant goal is
  reduced (lemma-driven, cheap) to the single residual `Γ(cast) x = x` — the global-sections image
  of the `Spec.map_comp φ ρ` glue. 5 distinct finishes tried, EACH failed for a named reason:
  `rfl` (not defeq — eqToHom carries content); `change`/`rfl` (**verified cold-build kernel bomb**
  — whnf deterministic timeout); element `simp` on `restrictScalarsComp'App_*` (discrimination-tree
  miss, the `X.Modules`/value-ModuleCat diamond); `erw [pushforwardComp_inv_app_val_app]` (doesn't
  fire — `moduleSpecΓFunctor.map g` not syntactically `g.val.app U`).
  **The residual is blocked on exactly TWO missing rw-only (kernel-light) exposure lemmas:**
  (a) `moduleSpecΓFunctor.map g` underlying `= (modulesSpecToSheaf.map g).val.app (op ⊤)`
  underlying; (b) `ConcreteCategory.hom (eqToHom _) x = x` for `ModuleCat`.
- **Crux `pullback_spec_tilde_iso_ring_square_natural` — NOT attempted** (foundation not fully
  landed; budget consumed). Correct deferral.

## SNAP `SectionGradedRing.lean` — 2 keystones closed; iter-010 object-fold blocker CLEARED; assembly STUCK
- **`sheafification_whiskerRight_unit_eq_mu'` / `_whiskerLeft_unit_eq_mu'` (NEW) — both SOLVED
  sorry-free** as defeq term-wrappers `:= sheafification_whiskerRight_unit_eq_mu P Q` (the pinned
  counit-object form is defeq to the unprimed; inferred `_` elaborates to it). lean_verify: no
  sorryAx.
- **`tensorObjAssoc_eq_localizedAssociator` — PARTIAL (1 `sorry`, 1→1).** iter-010's blocker is
  CLEARED: canonical keystones + `simp only [tensorObj]` make both residual `μ`'s print with
  IDENTICAL object args (verified via `lean_goal`); no `show`-pin needed. Two findings recorded:
  (1) `rw [show μ=μ from rfl]` is a **DEAD END** (`motive is not type correct` — μ object sits in a
  dependent whiskering-morphism type); `simp only [tensorObj]` sidesteps it. (2) The cancel STILL
  won't fire (`Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc` reported UNUSED): (a) ADJACENCY — the seg-1 keystone composite
  is atomic w.r.t. the outer `≫` (wrapped by `rw [Iso.eq_inv_comp, asIso_hom]`); (b) INSTANCE
  IDENTITY — `⊗_loc` not defeq `tensorObj` so the two μ's may not be the same `Iso` term to the
  matcher.
- **`hK_lhs`/`hK_rhs` — NOT scaffolded.** Gated on the explicit WELL-TYPED common form `K`: the
  planner-schematic `K = L'(α^p) ≫ μ⁻¹ ≫ (L'a ◁ μ⁻¹) ≫ (c_A ⊗ₘ ...)` does NOT typecheck (domain
  `sheafification.obj((a⊗b)⊗c)` ≠ assembly domain `sheafification.obj((toPresheaf)(A⊗B) ⊗ c)`; `K`
  must absorb counit object-glue on all 4 tensor slots). Prover declined a placeholder
  `K = assemblyRHS` (no isolation benefit).
- **5 gated cascade coherences** (`tensorPowAdd_assoc` ×2, `sectionMul_assoc_core` ×2,
  `sectionsMul_mul_assoc`, `tensorPowAdd_rightUnit`/`_braiding`) — unchanged, correctly not
  attempted (gated on the assembly).

## Subagent findings (reports in `.archon/logs/iter-011/`; triage → recommendations.md)
- **lean-vs-blueprint-checker snap011** — `[..-snap011.md]` FAITHFUL, **0 red flags.** Both
  `_eq_mu'` match their `\lean{}` targets + object-pinned forms; canonical-keystone blocks no
  overclaim (lean_verify sorry-free). The chapter **DOES** give the explicit common form `K`
  (L1500-1511, fully written, not schematic) — so SNAP is NOT blueprint-blocked; the typed-`K`
  derivation is prover-side work.
- **lean-vs-blueprint-checker fbc011** — `[..-fbc011.md]` statements all match targets; **blueprint
  prose for the residual is now CORRECT** (the iter-009/010 wrong "pointwise rfl" claim is fixed on
  the blueprint side, L880 "not a pointwise identity"). 1 major: the **Lean docstring** L754-755
  still carries the stale "pointwise reflexivity" claim. 2 minor: the two `:= rfl` helpers lack
  blueprint blocks.
- **lean-auditor iter011** — `[..-iter011.md]` 0 must-fix / 3 major / 5 minor; both files honest,
  sorries accurately documented. MAJOR: (1) misleading `gammaPushforwardNatIso_comp` docstring
  (hides the transitive sorry); (2) `set_option maxHeartbeats 4000000 in` @L1430 scopes to a
  `/-! -/` comment block, NOT to the Seam-2 decls; (3) 4-deep maxHeartbeats stack @L1465-1468
  cascades into a comment block — `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` @L1488 gets NO override
  (LATENT BUG: will time out once its sorry is filled). MINOR: fragile `erw`/`slice_rhs`+`erw`
  clusters (FBC L154; SNAP L1880/1885/2129); stale `iter-234/236` block comments (FBC L184-247).

## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
- **None.** No `\mathlibok` (no new Mathlib re-exports), no `\lean{}` rename (the prover's chosen
  names `_eq_mu'` / the FBC helpers already match the chapter or have no block), no stale
  `\notready`, no `\leanok` override.
- **`sync_leanok` attribution** (iter 11, sha 987aac7): added 2 `\leanok`, removed 0, touched ONLY
  `Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`. The SNAP canonical keystones carry statement `\leanok` that is
  **independently verified correct** (snap011 checker: lean_verify sorry-free; they are term-mode
  defs, so statement `\leanok` = fully done, no proof-block marker needed) — NO override applied.
  Minor attribution ambiguity (sync logged no SNAP touch despite the new decls) noted, not raised
  as CRITICAL per the sync-state guidance.

## Key findings / patterns
- **Kernel-light NatIso composition coherence** (FBC, reusable): reduce `Iso.ext` →
  `NatTrans.ext; funext` → STRUCTURAL `simp only` (whiskering/associator/`NatIso.ofComponents_hom_app`)
  → `exact` the per-component lemma. The associators become `𝟙` absorbed by cheap `id_comp`, NOT
  the sheaf `whnf` — keeps the kernel term small. Avoid `ext N x` (element expansion = bomb) and
  `change`/`rfl` defeq collapse (= cold-build whnf timeout).
- **μ-object fold via `simp only [tensorObj]`** (SNAP, reusable): folds the def to make
  object-identical μ's print identically; `rw [show μ=μ from rfl]` is a DEAD END (motive not type
  correct — μ in a dependent type).
- **Both `change`/`rfl`/`defeq` on Spec-sheaf carriers = cold-build kernel bomb** (re-confirmed,
  now with a verified per-component instance). Finishes must be `rw`/`erw`-only.

## Recommendations
See `recommendations.md`. Headline: FBC foundation is one bounded infra step from done (scaffold 2
named exposure lemmas) — prioritize; SNAP assembly is STUCK on the warm monolithic lane (3 iters,
0 net sorry) — pivot to the hK split + typed-`K` derivation, do NOT re-fire the warm lane.
